attacking_style: Early aggression specialist who commits to breakaways in the opening phases of races, prioritizing volume of attempts over selective timing for peak performance opportunities.
team_tactics: Appears to operate with freedom for opportunistic early moves rather than structured team-led setups; tactical approach emphasizes consistency over single-day peak form.
terrain_preference: Willing to engage on varied terrain in breakaway contexts, though specific climbing strength unclear; operates best when able to control effort distribution across multi-stage races.
vulnerability: Susceptible to overcooking early efforts, particularly in aggressive opening sequences on climbs; cramping issues suggest pacing discipline problems when committing to hard early moves.
key_patterns: Daily breakaway participation strategy across multi-stage races to accumulate classification points consistently.
Early race aggression followed by physical consequences; cramping after intense early efforts observed.
Volume-over-quality approach: multiple attempts rather than waiting for optimal single opportunity.
form_trajectory: negative: cramping and forced stops after early aggression indicate unsustainable pacing and possible fitness limitations despite achieving classification objectives
